meta_description: The three questions Tumisang asks every birthday — who am I, who do I pretend to be, who do I want to be — and what honest storytelling demands of us.

meta_description: Part two: the unsettling discovery that his toxic masculinity grew directly from trying not to be like the toxic men he grew up around — and what truthful living demands instead.

meta_description: One-on-one picnics with beloved friends, the loss of Bambi, and wrapping up a COVID-19 research project — August 2020 held both grief and quiet joy.

meta_description: Protests for racial justice, the end of Stanford Spring Quarter, and modelling COVID-19 in Botswana — a month of upheaval held with clear eyes.

title: Reflections From October 2020
meta_description: Job rejections and unexpected wins, holding joy and sorrow simultaneously — October 2020 as a crash course in living fully inside contradiction.

title: Reflections From September 2020
meta_description: Wildfires turned the sky orange, the pandemic surged, and Tumisang made the decision to leave academia for industry — September 2020 was apocalyptic and clarifying.

title: Reflections on Autumn 2021
meta_description: A surprise trip to Botswana for a community graduation celebration, a Canadian getaway, and abundant dinner parties — autumn 2021 as a season of harvest and homecoming.

title: Reflections on Q1 of 2021
meta_description: A quarterly check-in across the four pillars of a well-lived life: health, relationships, professional growth, and financial sustainability.

title: Reflections on Q2 of 2021
meta_description: Driving license secured, dream job landed, second Stanford engineering degree earned — Q2 2021 was the quarter everything came together.

title: Reflections on Summer 2021
meta_description: The first summer without school structure felt like the true beginning of adult life — spent working on a long-deferred personal writing project and learning to put himself first.

title: Retiring the Persona of Tumisang Ramarea
meta_description: A farewell to public writing life — reflecting on the shame of humble origins that drove the need to over-explain, and the healing that finally made it possible to stop.

meta_description: Combing through 18 years of crushes and relationships to understand a recurring pattern: the battle between ego and joy, and what a partner who wins that battle actually looks like.

title: Some Highlights From a Stressful Time
meta_description: Senior year at Stanford is stressful — but collaborative problem-solving in tough classes and the camaraderie it creates are among its unexpected gifts.

title: Some Reviews From Some Friends

meta_description: A practical note to young Botswana entrepreneurs: pricing should always incentivise the customer to buy more, not less — with egg math to prove it.

meta_description: Milk going bad becomes a case study in household inventory optimisation — a househusband-in-training applies his Management Science degree to the very unglamorous problem of groceries.

meta_description: The last undergraduate summer: living off campus in East Palo Alto for the first time, rediscovering the pleasure of commuting with a book, and beginning the path toward a home that is truly his own.

title: Thankful!
meta_description: A Thanksgiving dinner where the host did not cook a single thing — and why providing a platform for others to feed each other is its own kind of hospitality.

title: The Dawn Of A New Day

title: The Evolution of My Ambition
meta_description: A Facebook post from a former schoolmate prompts a trace of ambition's arc — from escaping poverty in Kanye to Stanford and beyond, and what drives a person to keep reaching.

title: The Great Meal
meta_description: A memorable meal and everything it meant.

title: The Inspiration
meta_description: A summer in a shared East Palo Alto house turns cooking into a daily spiritual practice — a meditation on presence, nourishment, and finding meaning in the simple act of preparing food.

title: The Last Flight to Washington DC: A Brief Reflection on Luck!
meta_description: Catching the very last flight into the US before a travel ban, after driving 1,600km across Botswana and narrowly missing a cow at 140km/h — a meditation on how thin the line between luck and catastrophe really is.

title: The Man and His Dreams
meta_description: A Vision Board made in 2014 listed Stanford as a dream — and became a prophecy. The story of how visualising a future can quietly make it real.

title: The Tragedy of Botho in Botswana
meta_description: Botho — the Tswana concept of shared humanity — is invoked by those in power to demand compliance, not justice. An essay on what Botho truly demands of us as a people.

title: The Zebra Has Earned Its Stripes
meta_description: Walking across Stanford's commencement stage as a poor widow's son from Kanye, Botswana — a celebration of the country's investment in education and a personal accounting of what it cost to get here.

title: "Two Worlds Uninhabited, Water and Fire"
meta_description: A birthday reflection on straddling Sengwaketse spirituality and Christianity, Botswana and California — and the poem that has always described his in-between life.

title: When Masisi Came To Visit
meta_description: Meeting President Masisi of Botswana in the Bay Area felt like a parent returning from the farm with sweet things from the harvest — and gave Tumisang renewed hope for home.

title: Where To From Here?
meta_description: Getting into the Stanford coterm program means one more year and a Master's degree — time to update the Vision Board and extend the strategic plan.

title: Who Said I Cannot Dance?
meta_description: Dressing for sunshine when the sky is overcast is an act of faith — and so is dancing at the Stanford SASA Fountain Party as if the whole universe rearranged itself just for you.

title: Who Was I? Who Am I? Who Will I Be?
meta_description: A childhood family photo and years of therapy raise the same stubborn question: how much do we really change, and is the smile we carried at five still the one we wear today?

title: A Thousand Lifetimes
meta_description: Inspired by Andrew Marvell, a short story of a chance encounter at a five-day conference that turns into something quietly magical — told from the Gaborone bus rank, still giggling.

title: …and Finally it Rained in California!
meta_description: A speculative fiction piece imagining Motlalepula, the greatest rainmaker in Botswana, descended from the first African missionaries who benevolently colonised the West.

title: Collection of Old Stories
meta_description: A set of short stories written between 2011 and 2013, using fiction to make sense of the world — the lived experiences of a young writer finding his voice.

title: Fa Le Phirima
meta_description: A Setswana short story of two people by the ocean at sunset — Kabo confessing to Merapelo that his heart is all he has to give, and finding that it is enough.

title: Homeless
meta_description: Standing at a street corner while a man waves flyers of a white Jesus, a narrator wrestles with mortality, organised religion, and the question of where any of us truly belong.

title: Just Talking
meta_description: The shortest of short stories: a DM, a friendship, a spark, a flame that burns itself out. No big deal. The birds chirp on.

title: Lehuma Leswe
meta_description: A Setswana story following Mothusi, a diligent student from Diphiring who quietly builds a small lending business to help classmates in need — proving the story's title: poverty is not a disgrace.

title: Mma Pelo
meta_description: A Setswana story of an elderly woman, Mma Pelo, left alone in a quiet homestead — a portrait of loneliness, the absence of family, and the small dignities of old age.

title: Se Ileng Se a bo Se Ile
meta_description: A Setswana story about Mompoloki, who studied abroad to lift his mother out of poverty, and returns home too late — she has already passed. What is gone is gone.

title: Take Off
meta_description: At OR Tambo Airport, Katlegо watches an Emirates plane and remembers a life of first-class travel with her now ex-boyfriend Troy — a short story about what we lose when love ends.

title: Tragic Endings
meta_description: Inspired by a true story and Eminem's song — a woman endures a charming but controlling partner's quiet cruelties, until the night she burns his gifts and reclaims herself entirely.
